exemplar.title: How does the resistance of the circuit consisting of a solenoid affect the exit velocity of a magnet falling through a copper tube?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the resistance of the circuit consisting of a solenoid affect the exit velocity of a magnet falling through a copper tube?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The research question is stated clearly and remains the focal point throughout, with consistent connection to each section.
Methodology is comprehensive, with wellâjustified choice of variables, detailed diagrams and a logical stepâbyâstep procedure.
Topic is communicated accurately and effectively, using technical definitions to maintain precision.
weaknesses: Minor digressions (personal anecdote, lengthy law quotations) slightly reduce academic concision.
Some control information lacks quantified justification, e.g. uncertainties in Table 1 without explicit note of negligible error.
Indentation in the controlâvariable list is inconsistent, affecting readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are small lapses in academic tone and formatting, the research question, context and methods are all communicated with clear purpose and focus, and the methodology is fully appropriate. These qualities justify the top band score of 6 in Focus and Method.

strengths: Consistent, accurate use of specialist terminology (eddy currents, RMSE, inverseâsquare resistance) throughout.
Strong application of subject knowledge in derivations, uncertainty propagation and error discussion.
Coherent integration of theory (electromagnetic induction, kinematics, Newtonâs laws) with experimental data.
weaknesses: Occasional typographical slips (âpermissivityâ vs âpermeabilityâ) affect conceptual precision.
A few abrupt transitions in the theoretical discussion (e.g. introduction of ÎT without defining Tâ) momentarily disrupt clarity.
Raw data rounding rationale is not explicitly explained.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates excellent understanding and application of HL-level physics and terminology. Minor conceptual slips and formatting inconsistencies prevent a perfect theoretical narrative, but the overall command of knowledge merits the maximum score of 6.

strengths: Data analysis is thorough, with linearisation, uncertainty quantification and comparison of experimental vs theoretical gradients.
Arguments are logically structured from hypothesis through to evaluation, supporting each claim with data evidence.
Evaluation section offers realistic improvements and discusses systematic vs random errors in depth.
weaknesses: Critical evaluation is largely confined to one section rather than woven continuously through the essay.
Some theoretical linkages (Faradayâs and Lenzâs laws to RQ) are paraphrased rather than critically analysed in context.
Gradientâuncertainty method could be formalised with standard error propagation rather than averaging differences.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates consistently strong analysis and coherent argumentation, with an insightful but slightly concentrated evaluation. This performance sits just below the absolute top band, warranting 11 out of 12 for Critical Thinking.

strengths: Structure follows IB conventions, with title page, contents, numbered sections, labelled figures and comprehensive bibliography.
Layout is clean, with consistent margins, typeface and aligned tables and figures.
Integration of equations and images is neat, supporting clarity of presentation.
weaknesses: Duplicate section numbers and occasional lowâresolution images interrupt visual polish.
Some figures (e.g. Figure 9) lack axis titles/units, reducing interpretability.
Minor inconsistencies in section symbols (e.g. âÂ§2â vs â\S2â) can confuse readers.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs structure and formatting adhere closely to IB expectations, with only small lapses in numbering and image quality. The effective layout and integration of elements justify the top Presentation score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Specialist terms (eddy currents, RMSE, inverse-square resistance) are used accurately throughout, variables are symbolised consistently, and equations are correctly formatted.  Occasional typographical slips ("permissivity" for "permeability") are minor; overall use is precise and sustained, fitting the top band but again not flawless.

The argument flows logically: theory â hypothesis â method â raw â processed â graphs â evaluation.  Each claim is supported by data and uncertainty reasoning, giving clear coherence.  Some sections (e.g., acceptance of hypothesis based solely on visual fit) show limited depth, preventing the level of sophistication needed for 10-12, but the structure and reasoning are clearly âgoodâ.

The methodology includes independent, dependent and six control variables with justification and quantified uncertainties, a full apparatus list, circuit diagram, step-by-step procedure (10 trials Ã 11 resistances) and precautions.  The methods are entirely appropriate to answer the RQ.  Some control descriptions (e.g. plastic lining) are a little qualitative and the source of the photogate calibration is not evidenced, so the strand merits 5 rather than 6.

The student applies electromagnetic induction, Newtonâs laws and kinematics, deriving a relationship, then comparing gradients to theoretical values.  Numerical substitution, uncertainty propagation and discussion of systematic vs random error illustrate excellent command of HL physics.  A few simplifications (neglect of self-inductance, assumption of zero friction in derivation) limit it from perfect coherence, so 5/6.

The student identifies two principal sources of systematic error (solenoid reconstruction, friction) and proposes realistic improvements plus an extension exploring temperature dependence.  Quantitative comparison of %error vs %uncertainty is given.  Evaluation is thoughtful but not woven âthroughoutâ the essay; it appears mainly in one section, and alternative interpretations of data are limited, placing it in the 4-6 band at the upper end.

The student states the phenomenon, context and purpose clearly from the opening paragraph and repeats it in a concise research-question sentence. Technical terms such as âexit velocityâ and âsolenoid resistanceâ are defined, diagrams are provided, and the essay remains consistently on that focus.  Minor verbosity and occasional digressions (personal anecdote, lengthy quotation of laws) prevent a flawless communication, so the strand fits comfortably in the upper band but not at the absolute maximum.

Layout is clean: consistent margins, typeface, embedded diagrams and tables aligned.  Occasional stretching of images and one table split awkwardly across pages reduce visual polish slightly, but the layout still supports communication effectively, meriting the higher band, lower score.

The research question is explicitly stated in bold twice and is narrow (only resistance, one magnet, one tube).  All later theory, data tables and plots are explicitly linked back to that question, demonstrating a strong sustained focus.  A small weakness is that the question switches wording between âvoltageâ and âresistanceâ early in the introduction, showing a minor lapse in precision, hence 5 not 6.

Data are extensive (100 raw points), processed with averages, percentage uncertainties and linearisation.  The student compares experimental and theoretical lines, calculates gradient uncertainty, intercept error, derives g, and contrasts systematic vs random error.  Analysis is clearly relevant and generally sound; however, it does not probe deeper modelling limitations (e.g. magnetic damping inside solenoid), so the work meets the 7-9 band mid-high level rather than 10-12.

The essay contains title page, contents, section numbering, labelled figures, tables with captions and correct citation list, following IB conventions.  Images and equations are integrated neatly.  Minor lapses (duplicate Â§ numbers, some images low resolution) keep it from perfection but overall presentation is clear and appropriate.

annotations.comment: The evaluation section insightfully identifies strengths of data collection speed and low uncertainty, showing good reflection on method effectiveness.
When discussing the extension, introduce the reference temperature Tâ explicitly before using ÎT to avoid ambiguity in experimental design.
References vary in style and completeness (missing DOIs, inconsistent retrieval dates). Adopt a consistent citation format (e.g., APA) throughout.
The inclusion of the word count at the start of the essay demonstrates adherence to IB formatting requirements, ensuring compliance with the 4 000-word limit.
The research title is prominently stated on the title page, establishing clear purpose and focus from the outset.
In the table of contents, â\S2 Theoryâ uses an inconsistent symbol rather than âÂ§2,â which may confuse readers. Consider standardizing section symbols.
The opening anecdote is engaging but reads as informal. Tighten the language to maintain academic tone and strengthen focus.
The research question refers to voltage across the solenoid but the investigation actually manipulates resistance. Ensure the wording remains precise and consistent.
Clear description of the solenoid placement below the tube and supporting diagram enhance understanding of experimental setup.
The variable Î¼â is described as the âpermissivityâ of free space; the correct term is âpermeability.â This slip affects the accuracy of conceptual understanding.
The phrase âwhere [continued on the next page]â breaks the flow and structure. Integrate variable definitions without interruption for clearer presentation.
The transition from the derived solenoid force equation to introducing the copper tube is abrupt. Consider summarizing the rationale more succinctly.
The theoretical discussion paraphrases Faradayâs and Lenzâs laws without directly linking them to the research question. Deepen the analysis to show relevance.
Statement âF_B = 0â as the magnet enters the solenoid contradicts earlier discussion of damping. Clarify assumptions about eddy currents inside the coil region.
The substitution leading to equation (5) introduces u without explicitly redefining it. Reinstate the definition to maintain clarity.
Section numbering repeats âÂ§3â for two different levels. Adjust headings to ensure unique, hierarchical numbering throughout.
Control-variable list uses uneven indentation, making it hard to parse reasons versus methods. Standardize list formatting for clarity.
Table 1 lists many items without uncertainties (dashes). If uncertainties are truly negligible, note this explicitly to justify omission.
Resistor tolerances (e.g. 2 Î© Â± 0.02 Î©) are unrealistically tight for standard parts. Verify component specs or adjust uncertainty calculations.
Procedure item 4 drops all trials at one resistance before moving to the next. Consider randomizing trial order to mitigate systematic drift effects.
Raw velocity data are recorded to 3 d.p. despite photogate precision of 5 d.p. State rationale for truncation to avoid confusion.
The processed-data table shows varying percentage uncertainties in 1/RÂ² with increasing R. Add a brief note explaining the nonâuniform behaviour.
Figure 9 is missing axis titles and units, which are essential for interpreting plotted experimental and theoretical data.
In Figure 10, the maximum and minimum gradient lines blend into the bestâfit line. Use distinct colours or line styles with a legend for clarity.
The method for determining gradient uncertainty averages differences rather than using a standard uncertainty formula. Consider applying propagation of error formally.
The unit âmÎ©Â² sâ»Â²â in the gradient expression is confusing. Use âmÂ²âsâ»Â²â for velocityâsquared axes or clarify notation consistently.
The conclusion refers to a âÎ½-interceptâ typo. Correcting this to ây-interceptâ will enhance readability and precision.
